insights.title: Separability of Capabilities
insights.description: Frozen-task accuracy and adaptive reasoning quality are entirely separable dimensions. High static performance provides limited signal for multi-turn adaptability.

insights.title: Instability as a Core Axis
insights.description: Trajectory instability is a distinct axis of model behavior. Models can exhibit varied levels of internal oscillation when traversing belief states, regardless of their final correctness.

insights.title: Beyond Standard Interactive Eval
insights.description: Counterfactual coherence forms a third orthogonal capability axis. A model's ability to selectively update structural beliefs while preserving invariants reveals deeper cognitive robustness than simple conversational adaptability.

failureModes.label: Overconfident Error
failureModes.description: The model rigidly clings to a prior static belief despite encountering explicit overriding rules within the context window.
failureModes.implication: Demonstrates structural rigidity masking as high conviction.

failureModes.label: Static/Dynamic Gap
failureModes.description: The model correctly parses the facts in a frozen zero-shot pass, but entirely loses the plot when the same facts are metered out across multiple shifting turns.
failureModes.implication: Exposes fragile representations that cannot withstand temporal spacing.

failureModes.label: Social Belief Confusion
failureModes.description: The model involuntarily adopts false beliefs expressed by characters in a mini-world, overwriting its own authoritative world state.
failureModes.implication: Reveals poor separation between semantic grounding and agentic simulation.

failureModes.label: Missed Contradiction
failureModes.description: The model silently accepts structurally contradictory evidence in parallel futures without flagging the impossibility.
failureModes.implication: Highlights fundamental 'contradiction blindness' in base context processing.
